description: Decode-time (T==1) Q4_K weight-GEMV for FFN gate/up, FFN down, and attn q/o projection. Covers the owned warp GEMV route (q4k_gemv_warp_kernel) and the scheduler-expressible coop / vdot variants.
exposed_instruction_primitives: udot4 / v_dot4 builtin (Q4K_VDOT) -- schedulable, NATIVE; but loses in-model 0.96x due to per-kernel q8 activation-quant cost (~7us)
exposed_scheduling_primitives: workgroup_shape (threads/row, K-block parallelism via coop rt)
exposed_dataflow_primitives: K-block split + partial sum (coop: q4k_coop_partial + sum)
exposed_runtime_primitives: per-role flag gating (Q4K_GEMV_WARP, Q4K_GEMV_WARP_DOWN, Q4K_GEMV_WARP_PROJ, Q4K_ATTN_QO_COOP, Q4K_VDOT)

newly_exposed_primitives.status: EXPOSED opt-in 2026-06-25 (WARP_REDUCE_LOWERING, default OFF) -- a generic Ops.REDUCE over a pow2 (<=32) WARP/GROUP_REDUCE lane axis auto-lowers to the ds_bpermute ladder (drops LDS+barrier), correct (sum/max). Was 'excluded / no renderer lowering' before.
newly_exposed_primitives.remaining_to_default_on: mixed reduce (serial-K + group, the real GEMV K=4096 shape) still declines -> LDS; then W==D vs owned warp GEMV (Milestone 6)

excluded_primitives.primitive: cross_lane_reduction (mixed serial+group reduce, e.g. real GEMV K=4096)
excluded_primitives.reason: pm_warp_reduce first pass handles only a single full-group lane axis; a K>group reduce (group + serial-K/group) still falls to the LDS tree. The serial-then-ladder split is the M6 follow-on.
excluded_primitives.used_by_winning_kernel: q4k_gemv_warp_kernel (extra/q4_k_gemv_primitive.py) -- shipped default-on for gate/up + down + (2026-06-25) q/o proj

correctness_gate: byte_exact (lossless work-decomposition; greedy 0 mismatches vs default)
lifecycle_authority_gate: clean W==D (extra/qk_decode_runtime_overhead.py) interleaved drift-cancelled A/B; gate >= +5%@ctx1024 OR +7%@ctx4096 (FFN) / positive byte-identical (proj)
classification_detail: Cross-lane reduction is now scheduler-emittable for both single-group AND mixed serial+group reduces (real K=4096), opt-in (WARP_REDUCE_LOWERING), correct -- the cross-lane codegen gap that blocked search is RESOLVED. Milestone 6 (2026-06-25) WIRED a scheduler-generated FFN gate/up GEMV (Q4K_GEMV_SCHEDULER) and W==D-compared it to the owned warp kernel: it TRAILS ~2x AND cross-lane is ~neutral vs the LDS tree (-0.1..-0.3%) because the reduce is NOT the bottleneck of a bandwidth-bound GEMV. So the search-vs-oracle gap is NO LONGER the reduce/cross-lane -- it is the Q4_K dequant lifecycle + coalesced packed-word loads + block-group-K. Those are the next search-space levers.
next_search_space_levers: fused packed-Q4_K dequant in tinygrad ops with coalesced word loads (vs the lazy fp16 dequant the scheduler arm fuses)
block-group-K / occupancy via opts so a scheduler GEMV gets the owned kernel's K-split
per-component cross-lane for UPCAST>1 vector reduces -- LOW priority (reduce is not the bottleneck)
promotion_status_note: Owned warp GEMV is shipped default-on (search_space_id=manual_oracle_not_search_generated). Q4K_GEMV_SCHEDULER + WARP_REDUCE_LOWERING are research-only/default-off; a scheduler GEMV does not yet challenge the owned kernel (the gap is dequant/memory, not the reduce).
